Sarah Chung

Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 7th (+70kg - Women)
Quick Facts

Although Sarah started doing Judo just over a year and a half ago, she's made quite a big impression in a short period of time. Through her work ethic and unquestioned dedication, Sarah is already ranked #1 in the United States in the Female +70kg Paralympic division. This past May, Sarah took 7th place at the IBSA World Games in Seoul, Korea. With that result, Sarah moved to #7 in the +70kg division in the new IBSA World Rankings. In February, Sarah took fifth place at the Paralympic World Cup in Eger, Hungary. She earned the Gold Medal at the USA Judo National President's Cup in Irving, TX last November. Sarah travels into the city from Crystal Lake 4 - 5 each week for practice. It takes her nearly five hours round trip using public transportation. She's totally committed to becoming the absolute best Judo player that she can be. At the USA Judo Senior National Championships last May in Reno, NV, Sarah took home a Bronze Medal. With that result, she qualified as an alternate for the 2014 IBSA World Judo Championships. Competing in her first ever Judo tournament, the Illinois Open State Judo Championships, she earned two Silver Medals. It was definitely a precursor of things to come. With solid results at a couple of upcoming international tournaments, Sarah has a fantastic opportunity to qualify for the 2016 Paralympic Games in Rio. Her upside is tremendous. At only 25 years old, she has a minimum of 15 years to compete at a very high, international level.

Major Judo Awards:

  • 2015 7th place - IBSA World Games
  • 2015 5th place - Paralympic Judo World Cup Eger, Hungary
  • 2015 Gold Medal - Barrington Judo Winter Classic Hawthorne Woods, IL
  • 2014 Gold Medal - USA Judo National President's Cup
  • 2014 Gold Medal - Minooka Halloween Judo Open
  • 2014 Bronze Medal - USA Judo Senior National Championships
  • 2014 Silver Medal, Silver Medal - Illinois Open State Judo Championships

bio.education: Victor J. Andrew High School (Tinley Park, Ill.) '07 Moraine Valley Community College ’15, Culinary Arts
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Cichocki began swimming at age five in 1993. Both of her sister were swimmers, influencing her to try the sport. She has an intellectual impairment that makes it difficult for her to process information and also experiences seizures. She made her senior international debut at the Toronto Parapan American Games where she won a gold and two bronze medals. She lists swim coach Jody Hottes as the most influential person in her career and her swim idol is Matt Grevers
  • Daughter of Edward and Cheryl Cichocki
  • Has two sisters, Janine and Kristen
  • Hobbies include spending time with her family, bowling, cycling, fishing, wakeboarding, travel and cooking.
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 7th (100m Backstroke S14 - Women), 11th (200m Freestyle S14 - Women), 12th (200m Individual Medley SM14 - Women)

Quick Facts
  • Joey is the son of Sylvia and John Cifelli
  • His mom is a Spanish teacher and dad is an iron worker
  • He has three older brothers: Johnny, Robby and Vinny (Joey says life at the Cifelli house was pretty wild growing up with four extremely competitive boys in the house, but that his brothers are his best friends.)
  • Joey’s favorite dive is 5154B (he spent a lot of time working on his twisting for 2016. He changed the way he wrapped his twist and spent weeks in the belts trying to get his arms in the right position)
  • He likes to watch a movie or listen to chill music between dives
  • For Joey, the hardest part about training is overcoming the mental aspect of the sport and says it is hard to picture himself beating some of the people he grew up idolizing
  • He has spent time with a sports psychologist to build his mental toughness
  • Away from diving, Joey enjoys playing other sports, spending time with his family and friends, travelling and going to music festivals
  • He is 50 percent Italian, 25 percent Mexican, 25 percent Cuban and speaks Spanish fairly well
  • Majoring in marketing and management at Purdue University
Career Highlights
  • 2017 World University Games team member

Quick Facts
  • Son of Michele Cinnamo-King and Charlie Cinnamo. Has two brothers, Chuck and Chris

  • Married to Kate Cinnamo and has two children, Tate and Tess

  • Born with a congenital limb deficiency of his right arm
  • Played on the football team at Morse High School and was a dual sport athlete at Luther College where he competed on the football and track and field
  • Competed in several arenas including various CrossFit competitions, Spartan races, the Arnold Sports Festival and the Working Wounded Games
  • Since attending a Paralympic talent camp in Chicago in April 2014, Cinnamo has emerged as one of the top field athletes on Team USA as an F46 shot put thrower

Quick Facts

HEIGHT 6' 3"     WEIGHT 225 lbs

Follow Adam on Twitter @AC_Slider

Clark submitted his athlete profile on the recruitment webpage at the suggestion of a friend and was invited to attend rookie camp in March 2011. He previously played football and ran track at Centre College in Danville, Ky., where he earned all-conference honors in both sports. Clark graduated from Centre College in 2007, with a B.S. in Physics and then earned a B.S. in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Kentucky in 2009.

Clark enjoys spending time with his friends, going hiking, biking, rock climbing or "half a dozen other random sports." His favorite food is his wife Ashley's meatloaf, favorite music is a mix of rock, hip hop and country and favorite location is home on the farm.

Graduated from Centre College in 2007, with a B.S. in Physics and then earned a B.S. in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Kentucky in 2009

FUN FACTS

- He grew up on a family farm that his dad still operates.
- His wife is an active-duty Air Force dentist. 
- He can speak German.

  • 2011-2012 America's Cup as push athlete for Cory Butner in four-man - GOLD in Park City (11/11), GOLD in Lake Placid #1 (3/12), GOLD in Lake Placid #2 (3/12)

THE BIG BLURB
“Carson has the opportunity to become a good player that can start.” - U.S. Head Coach John Speraw in the Daily Pilot

THE QUOTE
“Everybody on the team is there to win and to represent Team USA in the best way possible. In volleyball, you can’t win with just one guy and I think that’s a good thing because you’re a little more humble. We’re hungry and ready to be the best in the world.” - Carson Clark

Quick Facts
  • Son of William Claye and Saffie Tunis
  • Has two siblings, Taiyou and James
  • Trains at the U.S. Olympic Training Center in Chula Vista, California

  • Skipped his final semester of high school to enroll early at Oklahoma
  • Two-time Arizona state champion in the triple jump
  • First Arizona high school athlete to triple jump more than 50 feet
  • He and Christian Taylor, fellow Olympian and former University of Florida teammate, both won medals at the 2012 Olympic Games
  • In 2012, became the first man since 1936 and the first American since 1904 to win Olympic medals in both the long and triple jumps
  • Married hurdler Queen Harrison
  • Enjoys music artist and fashion
  • Platinum recording artist
  • CEO of his own fashion label "Elevate."
